Accessibility at Microsoft

Our goal is to make accessibility easier for everyone. We are committed to driving responsible innovation and advancing shared progress so that accessible, trusted AI benefits everyone.
Explore skilling resources

Microsoft Ability Summit

Ability Summit brought together a global community of experts to help shape the future of AI and accessibility.
Watch highlights from Ability Summit 2026 Read about it on LinkedIn
End of Featured stories and announcements slideshow: navigate using the slide tabs section

Help when and where you need it

Answer your questions with AI

Ask Microsoft Accessibility (AskMA) is a tool that helps you quickly and independently find answers about accessibility features in Microsoft products and services.
Try it now

Disability Answer Desk

Where customers with disabilities can easily get support with Microsoft 365, Windows, Xbox, and Surface.
Get support via chat, phone, or ASL

AI skills

Explore accessibility training and resources
General

AI and accessibility overview

This video focuses on the intersection of AI and accessibility, discussing Microsoft's guidelines for responsible AI and the benefit to the disability community.

Duration: 4 minutes 30 seconds

Learn about AI solutions
People with disabilities

AI Skills for Accessibility

Learn how to use AI for everyday tasks work and creativity. This course is taught by people with disabilities and shares practical accessible ways to use AI confidently.

Duration: 1 hour

Learn about AI skills for people with disabilities

What’s new in accessibility at Microsoft?

Ability Summit 2026

Microsoft Ability Summit brought together a global community of experts to help shape the future of AI and accessibility.
Watch Ability Summit 2026 sessions Read about it on LinkedIn

The expanding role of assistive technology

More than 70% of consumers say assistive technology (AT) is essential to their daily work. Learn about Forrester's latest research on the impact of AT in the era of AI.
Read the global Forrester study

Our new Chief Accessibility Officer

Neil Barnett brings decades of experience in accessibility, inclusive technology, and disability advocacy to his new role as Chief Accessibility Officer.
Read the announcement

Microsoft’s commitment to the European Accessibility Act

Learn how we prepared for the European Accessibility Act, investing in updates across products like Windows, Teams, and Outlook to align with EU accessibility standards.
Read the EAA blog

Building trust

Guided by the values of respect, integrity, and accountability, we are committed to driving responsible innovation, building safe and secure technology, and advancing shared progress.

Learn about our commitment
